title: hieracium murorum subsp. rhacophyllum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. rhacophyllum is a subspecies of Hieracium murorum, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is known to have medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Hieracium murorum subsp. rhacophyllum have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, offering health benefits such as antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
